text:
This book offers a systematic analysis of the war between Russia and Ukraine, in particular the fighting power displayed by the warring parties during the first year of the all-out war launched by Russia on 24 February 2022. The war between Russia and Ukraine had already started in 2014, which, more than ten years on, has by no means ceased to be important; but with the escalation of 24 February 2022, it suddenly and powerfully drew world attention to a type of warfare that has not been seen in Europe in decades: high-intensity interstate conventional warfare.

This piece is not intended to be a comprehensive history of the war in the eastern Ukraine; instead, we are attempting a preliminary cross-domain assessment of how the capabilities of the Ukrainian and Russian armed forces evolved during the fighting and how they performed in combat. We start by describing the strategic and operational conceptions underlying the Ukrainian and Russian armies and their degree of continuity or divergence from the Soviet military doctrines inherited from the Cold War period.

This study assesses the warfighting potential of the land, air, maritime and cyber domains in contemporary conflict. It also examines the importance of international allied support, sanctions and Western-supplied weaponry for the Ukrainian military’s ability to withstand Russian attack. Finally, it assesses the place of inter-state war in the contemporary strategic environment and its potential future development. This study will be of particular interest to students and scholars of military and strategic studies, defence policy, international relations, as well as Russian and Eastern European affairs.
